items.title: Matplotlib object hierarchy for a single plot
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  fig["Figure — canvas container"]
  fig --> ax["Axes — one plot region"]
  ax --> artists["Artists — lines, scatters, text, patches"]
  pyplot["pyplot functions"] --> fig
  pyplot --> ax
items.caption: pyplot is a convenience layer; Figure holds Axes; Axes methods create drawable Artists.

items.title: Typical ML EDA plotting workflow
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  data["NumPy / pandas arrays"] --> create["fig, ax = subplots()"]
  create --> plot["scatter / plot / hist / bar"]
  plot --> formatting["labels, legend, alpha, figsize"]
  formatting --> output["show() or savefig()"]
items.caption: Same pipeline applies across basic plot types; only the Axes method changes.

items.title: State-based pyplot vs object-oriented style
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  state["plt.plot + plt.xlabel"] --> same["Same Figure and Axes objects"]
  oo["ax.plot + ax.set_xlabel"] --> same
  same --> render["Renderer draws to file or screen"]
items.caption: Both styles target the same objects; OO style scales better to subplots and reusable functions.
